commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bcbc0e3d2a22db1f693c87337dab7e68705d541f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens trusted tool policy execution by validating policy registrations, decisions, params, and approval payloads, with host-hook contract tests for malformed policy data and optional undefined fields.

PR surface: Source +373, Tests +310. Total +683 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main has unguarded trusted-policy registry, evaluation, and diagnostic reads, and the PR tests encode those malformed-data cases. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because a compatibility-sensitive fail-closed security decision remains plus one concrete sibling diagnostic-path repair.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- fails closed when trusted tool policy registration/list access, policy evaluation, decision fields, adjusted params, or approval payloads are unreadable/malformed
- preserves valid no-op optional-field shapes like `{ allow: undefined }` and approval options set to `undefined`
- keeps the slice narrow to trusted policy execution plus contract coverage with synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures only

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 1 shard, 71 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- touched-file scrub for the reported plugin/tool names, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean after fixing approval-shape and optional-undefined findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_d35e92e885d6`, lease `cbx_ebb6764b2560`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: trusted tool policy failures no longer fail open or crash policy evaluation when policy registries, registrations, decisions, params, or approval payloads are malformed/unreadable; malformed concrete decisions block deterministically while valid optional `undefined` no-op fields remain compatible.

Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree rebased on `origin/main` `a2b2c4a76c7`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused host-hooks contract test file, formatter check, diff check, scrub, autoreview until clean, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: contract coverage proves unreadable trusted policy lists/registrations/decisions/params/approval payloads and malformed concrete decisions return deterministic block results; valid optional-field no-op decisions and approval payloads with undefined optional fields still pass through without denial.

Observed result after fix: a bad trusted-policy payload blocks only the unsafe tool call with a clear policy reason instead of silently allowing malformed decisions or crashing policy evaluation.

What was not tested: no live Telegram/UI round trip for this policy-only slice; runtime behavior is covered through plugin host-hook contract tests and the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
